debugPanel.title: Debug Panel
debugPanel.pausedDescription: Execution paused at breakpoint. Use controls to continue.
debugPanel.noBreakpointsLive: Click on the left edge of a node to add a breakpoint
debugPanel.noBreakpointsNormal: Switch to Live mode to add breakpoints

traceStepInfo.title: Step details
traceStepInfo.empty: Select a step to view details
traceStepInfo.name: Name:

flowBreak.intro: The Break node interrupts loop execution.
flowBreak.item1: Used inside For Each and While loops
flowBreak.item2: Interrupts the loop immediately when executed
flowBreak.item3: Control continues from the loop's Completed output
flowBreak.note: Note: this node has no output pins because execution continues from the parent loop Completed output.

flowDelay.label: Delay in milliseconds (default)
flowDelay.intro: The Delay node pauses graph execution.
flowDelay.item2: You can set the delay via the pin or in settings
flowDelay.item3: If the value is negative or invalid, the delay becomes 0 ms
flowDelay.note: Note: delay is asynchronous and does not block other application processes.

flowForEach.intro: The For Each loop iterates over every array element.
flowForEach.item1: Connect an array to the Array input
flowForEach.item2: Use the Element output to get the current element
flowForEach.item3: Use the Index output to get the current index
flowForEach.item4: The loop body runs for each element
flowForEach.item5: The Completed output fires after the loop finishes
flowForEach.note: Note: you can interrupt the loop with the Break node.

flowWhile.intro: The While loop runs while its condition is true.
flowWhile.item1: Connect a boolean value to the Condition input
flowWhile.item2: Use the Iteration output to get the current iteration number
flowWhile.item3: The loop body runs on each iteration
flowWhile.item4: The Completed output fires after the loop finishes
flowWhile.warning: ⚠️ Important: the maximum iteration count is 1000. Make sure the condition can become false, otherwise the loop will be interrupted.
flowWhile.note: Note: you can stop the loop early with the Break node.

mathRandom.note: Note: if min or max contains a dot or comma, a floating-point number will be generated; otherwise it will be an integer.

whatIfEditor.title: Execution paused
whatIfEditor.editHint: Edit values directly on nodes - click on the green badges with data
whatIfEditor.syncHint: All changes are synchronized in real-time between users
whatIfEditor.changesWarning_one: ⚠️ You modified {{count}} value. Click "Continue" to apply changes.
whatIfEditor.changesWarning_other: ⚠️ You modified {{count}} values. Click "Continue" to apply changes.

breakpointDialog.description: Breakpoint will pause execution at this node. You can view and modify variable values before continuing.
breakpointDialog.conditionHelp: JavaScript expression. Leave empty for unconditional stop.
breakpointDialog.howItWorks1: Execution will stop only if condition returns true
breakpointDialog.howItWorks2: Use user to check user (username, id)
breakpointDialog.howItWorks3: Use args to check command arguments
breakpointDialog.howItWorks4: Use variables to check graph variables
breakpointDialog.exampleCount: Stop if number is greater than 10:
